problem_statement: Autonomous coding agents now submit AI-generated pull requests at massive scale, but human-centric code review has not scaled to match, creating a growing imbalance between agentic PR submission rates and review capacity that threatens quality assurance and accountability.
magnitude_evidence: 456,535 Agentic-PRs by five agents across 61,453 repositories and 47,303 developers; OpenAI Codex alone created over 400,000 PRs in under two months; one developer submitted 164 Codex-assisted PRs in 3 days vs. 176 human PRs over 3.5 years
insufficiency_evidence: Agentic-PRs are reviewed/closed much faster (Codex accepted PRs in 0.3h vs human 3.9h) yet 'this rapid pace may suggest superficial review rather than genuine confidence... it remains uncertain whether these Agentic-PRs undergo meaningful scrutiny or are merely triaged'; 58.2% of Agentic-PRs receive no explicit review; provider-aligned review bots may 'overlook stylistic issues or latent errors'
solution_mechanisms: route low-risk PRs to bots, escalate high-impact to senior reviewers
reviewer recommender systems tuned for workload
governance models for agent autonomy and accountability
cross-provider review bots for diverse evaluation
rule-based static analysis for security review
